steps.title: Identify what makes an effective introduction
steps.content: To introduce the Slow Food movement to a new audience, the introduction must convey what the movement fundamentally stands for—its core values and purpose—rather than just when or by whom it was founded.

steps.title: Match the core values to the choice
steps.content:
Choice A efficiently captures all four major values: universal access to food, high-quality production, sustainable practices, and fair worker treatment.

Choices B, C, and D either skip the movement's actual purpose (B focuses on dates), address only one value (C), or miss a key pillar (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
